raw_persona.narrative:
name: Theodora Tong
age: 19
sex: Female
marital_status: never_married
education_level: 9th_12th_no_diploma
occupation: not_in_workforce
hobbies_and_interests: digital illustration, watercolor painting, charcoal sketching, photography, poetry writing, journaling, reading literary fiction, reading philosophy, visiting art museums, nature walks, meditation, yoga, ambient music listening, volunteering in community art workshops, DIY bookbinding, indoor herb gardening, basic coding for interactive art
skills_and_expertise: digital illustration, watercolor painting, charcoal drawing, photography, poetry writing, creative nonfiction, critical analysis, research, active listening, empathy, peer mediation, basic first aid, mental health first aid, Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, basic Python scripting, HTML/CSS, WordPress site management, budget management, Spanish (conversational)
complementary_info: Theodora Tong grew up in Schenectady in New York’s Capital Region and comes from a European American family with Irish and English roots. Local life around the Mohawk River, the historic Stockade District, nearby museums and galleries, and seasonal traditions such as the Tulip Festival shaped her long-term attachment to quiet natural settings, heritage, volunteerism, and community service; family holidays like Thanksgiving and Christmas often center on homemade meals and storytelling. She keeps a solitary, reflective routine: journaling by hand, sketching by candlelight until she loses track of time, meditating, attending weekly gentle yoga classes at the community center, and sometimes forgetting to water her indoor herb garden on rainy days. She often walks along the Mohawk River, visits the Schenectady Museum, volunteers in small art-craft sessions for seniors, and plans low-cost trips using student discounts and hostels, especially to the Adirondack foothills; she has also taken interest in places like Montreal’s Old Port and museums in Washington, D.C. In her art life, she listens to Brian Eno while painting, studies artists such as Vincent van Gogh, Yayoi Kusama, and Georgia O’Keeffe, photographs urban textures and seasonal landscapes, and curates a social-media gallery of charcoal sketches inspired by the Stockade District. Her cooking follows a calm domestic rhythm, with recurring dishes like rosemary roasted potatoes, lemon-basil quinoa bowls, and strawberry-rhubarb crisp using basil, thyme, and garlic from her herb garden. She is building toward art therapy and community arts education through community college planning, volunteer workshop experience, and an emerging online print shop, with a long-term goal of opening a nonprofit art space in Schenectady for youth, seniors, and people facing mental-health challenges.

structured_persona.name: Theodora Tong
structured_persona.persona_summary: Theodora Tong is a quiet, introspective creator who channels compassion into solitary art‑therapy practice, meticulously budgets their student life while occasionally splurging on premium brushes, and they habitually lose track of time sketching by candlelight, often forgetting to water their herb garden on rainy days.
structured_persona.professional_persona: Theodora Tong, a 19‑year‑old aspiring art therapist, blends digital illustration proficiency, empathetic peer‑mediation, and meticulous yet flexible planning to design soothing therapeutic art workshops for seniors while juggling community college courses, volunteer art‑craft sessions, and an emerging online print boutique, and they channel their quiet determination into every canvas.
structured_persona.sports_persona: Theodora Tong enjoys low‑impact fitness, attending weekly gentle yoga classes at the community center, hiking the Adirondack foothills with the Adirondack Trailblazers club in spring, and quietly rooting for the Albany Great Danes basketball team, the New York Yankees, and the New York Knicks during the respective seasons, and they favor solitary, reflective movement over crowded gym environments.
structured_persona.arts_persona: Theodora Tong immerses themselves in visual creation, drawing inspiration from Vincent van Gogh’s emotive palettes, Yayoi Kusama’s immersive patterns, and Georgia O’Keeffe’s serene still‑lifes, while listening to ambient composer Brian Eno during watercolor sessions at the Schenectady Museum and curating a social‑media gallery of charcoal sketches that echo the historic Stockade District’s textures, and they cherish the quiet solitude that fuels their artistic curiosity.
structured_persona.travel_persona: Theodora Tong prefers solitary, contemplative trips, meticulously planning weekend escapes to the Adirondack foothills, cultural strolls through Montreal’s Old Port, and museum visits in Washington, D.C., while dreaming of a spring pilgrimage to Kyoto’s zen gardens and an autumn trek across the Scottish Highlands, always using student discounts and budget hostels to stretch their limited funds, and they relish the quiet reflection each new landscape provides.
structured_persona.culinary_persona: Theodora Tong enjoys moderately skilled home cooking, regularly preparing rosemary‑infused roasted potatoes, lemon‑basil quinoa bowls, and a seasonal strawberry‑rhubarb crisp topped with a scoop of vanilla yogurt, using fresh basil, thyme, and garlic harvested from their indoor herb garden, and they find the rhythmic chopping and simmering a soothing ritual that complements their meditative practice.
structured_persona.cultural_background: Theodora grew up in Schenectady, a small city in the Capital Region of New York with a rich Dutch colonial history and a vibrant contemporary arts scene. Her family is of European American descent, with roots tracing back to Irish and English ancestors who settled in the Northeast generations ago. The local culture—marked by seasonal celebrations such as the Tulip Festival, community gatherings at the historic Stockade District, and a strong emphasis on public education and volunteerism—shaped her values of community service and appreciation for heritage. The proximity to the Mohawk River and the surrounding countryside fostered her love for quiet natural settings, while the city's museums, galleries, and the nearby University at Albany exposed her to diverse artistic and intellectual influences. She observes traditional American holidays like Thanksgiving and Christmas, often incorporating homemade meals and family storytelling, and she participates in local volunteer initiatives, reflecting a cultural emphasis on civic responsibility and mutual support.
structured_persona.skills_and_expertise: Theodora has cultivated a blend of artistic and interpersonal skills through self‑directed learning and volunteer work. She is proficient in digital illustration using tools such as Procreate and Adobe Photoshop, and she practices watercolor and charcoal drawing. Her photography focuses on natural and urban landscapes, emphasizing composition and mood. She writes poetry and short reflective prose, and she excels at critical reading and research, often digging deep into artistic movements and psychological concepts. Her strong empathy and active‑listening abilities make her effective in peer mediation and volunteer counseling. She also possesses solid digital literacy, including Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, basic Python scripting for creative projects, and web‑design basics using HTML/CSS and WordPress. Additionally, she is trained in basic first aid and mental‑health first‑aid, enabling her to support others in crisis.
structured_persona.hobbies_and_interests: Theodora spends much of her free time immersed in solitary creative pursuits. She enjoys experimenting with digital illustration, watercolor, and charcoal sketching, often drawing inspiration from nature walks along the Mohawk River and the rolling hills of the nearby Adirondack foothills. She keeps a handwritten journal where she writes poetry, reflections, and short stories. Photography of urban textures and seasonal landscapes allows her to capture quiet moments. She is an avid reader of literary fiction, philosophy, and art theory, and she frequently visits the Schenectady Museum and local galleries for quiet inspiration. Meditation, gentle yoga, and listening to ambient instrumental music help her maintain inner balance. Occasionally, she volunteers at the community center, leading small art‑craft sessions for seniors, which aligns with her desire to help others while staying in a calm environment. She also enjoys learning basic coding for interactive art, exploring DIY bookbinding, and tending a small indoor herb garden.
structured_persona.career_goals_and_ambitions: Theodora aims to merge her artistic talent with her deep compassion by pursuing a career in art therapy or community arts education. In the next two years she plans to enroll in a local community college to earn an associate’s degree in Visual Arts while completing a certificate in mental‑health first aid. She intends to transfer to a four‑year university for a Bachelor’s in Psychology with a concentration in therapeutic arts, followed by graduate certification as an Art Therapist. She also hopes to build a professional portfolio of digital illustrations and sell prints online to support herself. Long‑term, she dreams of establishing a nonprofit art space in Schenectady that offers free workshops, quiet studio time, and therapeutic art programs for youth, seniors, and individuals facing mental‑health challenges.

test_sessions.instruction: For a solo outbound trip on 2026-09-18, I need to get from New York, NY to Montreal, QC. It's for a couple of quiet days of museum visits and walking around the Old Port, so please check the available options and book it.

rule_ref.rule_text: For regional or contemplative trips, she prefers ground transport over flying.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: For trips I can reasonably do over land, I’d rather take ground transport than fly.

test_sessions.instruction: I'll be in Porto and need a place to stay. Please choose one option in Porto with 1 room for 1 guest and book it.

rule_ref.rule_text: When booking lodging, she prefers budget hostels over more conventional hotel stays.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: I usually choose a budget hostel if it helps me stretch the trip affordably.

test_sessions.instruction: Please book the lowest-priced flight from Chicago to Minneapolis for just me from the available options. I’m only taking a backpack, so use the traveler details and payment method on file and go ahead once you find the best match.

rule_ref.rule_text: When she does fly, she prefers a window seat for the view and reflective travel time.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: If I’m on a plane, I want the window seat.

test_sessions.instruction: I'm starting from scratch on a first-time Kyoto trip for myself: 3 days, solo. From the candidate stops below, pick one place to anchor the itinerary and build the plan around it.

rule_ref.rule_text: When planning a trip, she prefers stops centered on museums, gardens, and quiet nature rather than nightlife or high-energy attractions.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: I plan trips around museums, gardens, and quiet scenic places, not nightlife.

test_sessions.instruction: One stop on the Kyoto trip fell through. Please swap in a single replacement for a solo daytime visit somewhere that still fits this part of the day, and keep the route practical around the eastern Kyoto portion of the itinerary.

rule_ref.rule_text: If a trip stop falls through, she prefers replacement stops that preserve the original quiet cultural or nature theme rather than swapping in any generic top-rated alternative.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: When a stop changes, I want the replacement to keep the same calm museum-or-nature feel.

test_sessions.instruction: Please write something up for the seniors' art-craft group I lead at the community center. Save it in /CommunityCenter/Art Programs, title it "Button Collage Session Materials," and include the supply list, the room setup, and the steps for the activity.

rule_ref.rule_text: When setting up materials for recurring art sessions, she prefers a reusable template rather than starting from scratch each time.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: For recurring workshop materials, I like a template I can reuse.

attributes.title: Button Collage Prep Notes
attributes.content_brief: One-page outline for a senior button collage activity covering supplies, table arrangement, and the order of the craft steps.

attributes.title: Volunteer Reminder: Watercolor Table Setup
attributes.content_brief: Preparation instructions for cups, brushes, aprons, and seating before a watercolor group for older adults.

attributes.title: Tissue Flower Activity Outline
attributes.content_brief: Short write-up for a flower-making session with a materials list, setup notes, and activity flow.

attributes.title: Spring Senior Art Hour Recap
attributes.content_brief: Summary of attendance, favorite projects, and supply usage from recent community-center art gatherings.

attributes.title: Senior Craft Session Planner
attributes.content_brief: Blank layout with sections for supplies, room setup, activity steps, and cleanup notes for community-center craft groups.

test_sessions.instruction: Please put together something on color theory and visual attention in painting, save it in /Writing/Research, and title it "Color, Attention, and Mood in Painting".

rule_ref.rule_text: When organizing research on art theory or psychology, she prefers a report format rather than an informal note.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: For deeper research writeups, I prefer a report so the thinking is organized.

attributes.title: Color Theory Reading Notes
attributes.content_brief: Passages, page references, and short summaries from books on color, contrast, and composition.

attributes.title: Visual Attention Source Notes
attributes.content_brief: Collected takeaways from psychology articles on attention, salience, and perception.

attributes.title: Aesthetics Reading Circle Memo
attributes.content_brief: Brief recap of discussion topics from a small reading group on aesthetics and perception.

attributes.title: Symbolism and Viewer Response
attributes.content_brief: Comparative synthesis of sources on symbolism, archetypes, and audience interpretation.

test_sessions.instruction: Please sort the files sitting in /Desktop/Current — it's a mix of images, PDFs, spreadsheets, and docs from the last few months, and I just need the folder organized.

rule_ref.rule_text: When organizing creative and school files, she prefers them to be classified by project rather than by file type or date.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: I organize files by project so my workshop, class, and art pieces stay grouped together.
